Interface ServerConnection

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
RmiServerConnection, SocketServerConnection
public interface ServerConnection extends AutoCloseable
Commands the client can send to the server, plus the lifecycle hooks (handler registration and close) used by the transport.

Decouples the client from the concrete transport (socket / RMI): implementations only need to provide send(ClientRequest), setMessageDispatcher(ServerHandler) and close(). All command methods are default and build the appropriate ClientRequest in one place, so adding a new command requires editing only this file plus the corresponding request record.

Most game requests carry a matchId — provided by the server in response to createMatch(String, int) or addPlayerToLobby(String, String).
